name: Cranberry Pear Tart
recipeIngredient: 3 cups Fresh or frozen cranberries (thaw if frozen)
1 Firm, ripe pear, pared, cored and chopped (about 1 cup)
2/3 cup Finely chopped walnuts (or finely ground)
recipeInstructions:
From "The Family Circle Christmas Treasury."
Combine the water, 3/4 cup sugar, and red currant jelly in a medium-size saucepan. Place over medium heat until the jelly melts and the sugar dissolves. Bring to boiling point. Add cranberries and pear pieces. Cook, uncovered, over medium heat until cranberries pop and mixture thickens slightly, about 10-15 minutes. Cool completely.
Preheat oven to 375°F. In a medium bowl, cut butter into flour mixture until crumbly. Mix 1/4 cup sugar, chopped walnuts (or ground), and cinnamon. Add egg yolks. Mix lightly with fork until pastry holds together and side of bowl is clean. Press dough over bottom and sides of a 9-inch tart pan with removable bottom. Prick the bottom with fork. Refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Bake shell in preheated oven at 375°F for 12-15 minutes, or until golden brown. Cool completely. Spoon cooled cranberry filling into cooled pastry shell. Preheat oven to 400°F.
Beat egg whites and cream of tartar until soft peaks form. Gradually beat in sugar until stiff but not dry, about 1 cup. Spread half meringue over top of tart. Pipe remaining meringue in lattice pattern. Bake at 400°F for 3-6 minutes, or until golden brown. Decorate with additional cranberries if desired. Makes 8 servings.
Nutritional analysis per serving: 418 calories; 17.7 grams total fat (8 grams saturated); 5.6 grams protein; 22.8 grams carbohydrates; 98 milligrams cholesterol; 152.2 milligrams sodium.
